evals.prompt: Create a module called `restricted_reports` for our Drupal 10 Open Social site. It should define a custom permission 'view restricted reports' and provide a route at /reports/restricted that shows a simple page. Only users with the 'view restricted reports' permission should be able to access it. Anonymous users should get a 403 Access Denied response. Use AccessResult objects for access checking, not bare booleans. We want D11 compatibility.
evals.expected_output: permissions.yml, route with _permission, controller with AccessResult
expectations: Permission defined in restricted_reports.permissions.yml with 'title' key (permission string must match what route uses)
Route _permission requirement references the EXACT permission string defined in .permissions.yml
If custom access checking code exists, it returns AccessResult objects (AccessResult::allowed(), AccessResult::forbidden(), AccessResult::neutral()) -- NOT bare booleans (TRUE/FALSE)
If AccessResult objects are used, they include ->addCacheContexts(['user.permissions']) or ->addCacheTags() for cache-aware access
Does NOT use hook_permission() (D7 pattern) -- permissions are in .permissions.yml
Does NOT implement manual CSRF token checking -- would use _csrf_token route option if CSRF protection needed
The module enables successfully: ddev drush en restricted_reports returns exit code 0
E2E: Anonymous gets 403 -- navigate to /reports/restricted as anonymous and verify access denied
E2E: Admin gets 200 -- navigate to /reports/restricted as admin and verify page loads
